LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JUILETTE VO, CHIS JAMES CORPORATION AND PAULA GOLD-NOCELLA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE LISTING AGREEMENT STATES THAT THE PARTIES AGREE TO MEDIATE PRIOR TO COMMENCING SUIT OR ARBITRATION; HOWEVER, IF A PARTY FAILS TO DO SO, THAT PARTY IS NOT ENTITLED TO RECOVER ATTORNEY'S FEES. (FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC"), EX. A.) THEREFORE, THE CONTRACT PROVIDES THAT THE REMEDY FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THIS CONTRACTUAL PROVISION IS LOSS OF ATTORNEY'S FEES, NOT BARRING THE CLAIMS. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT ROBERT CARTER IS AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY TO THE SUIT. ALTHOUGH MR. CARTER EXECUTED THE LISTING AGREEMENT, HE EXECUTED THE CONTRACT AS AN AGENT ON BEHALF OF A PRINCIPAL, VANGUARD PROPERTIES. WHEN AN AGENT EXECUTES A CONTRACT ON BEHALF OF A DISCLOSED PRINCIPAL, THE PRESUMPTION IS THAT THE PRINCIPAL AND NOT THE AGENT IS TO BE BOUND. (BANK OF AMERICA NAT. TRUST AND SAVINGS ASS'N V. CRYER (1936) 6 CAL.2D 485, 489.) BECAUSE THE FAC SEEKS RELIEF FROM VANGUARD PROPERTIES, AS THE PRINCIPAL, AND NOT FROM MR. CARTER, AS THE AGENT, MR. CARTER IS NOT AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY. PERSONS AGAINST WHOM NO RELIEF IS SOUGHT CANNOT PROPERLY BE JOINED AS DEFENDANTS. (PINNACLE HOLDINGS INC. V. SIMON (1995) 31 CAL.APPL.4TH 1430, 1437.) ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JUILETTE VO, CHIS JAMES CORPORATION AND PAULA GOLD-NOCELLA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO EV. CODE SEC. 452(H). THE FOLLOWING PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") ARE STRICKEN PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 436(A): PAR. 13, PG. 5, LINES 22-24; PAR. 18, PG. 7, LINES 8-14; PAR. 1, PG. 1, LINE 27 - PG. 2, LINE 2; PAR. 13, PG. 5, LINE 28 - PG. 6, LINE 1; PAR. 18, PG. 7, LINES 6-7. THE FAC DOES NOT PLEAD A STATUTORY OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. ADDITIONALLY, THE OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO STRIKE DID NOT SET FORTH ANY ARGUMENT EXPLAINING WHY THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE APPLIES. THE FAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH "OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OR MALICE" TO SUPPORT A PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. (CIV. CODE SEC. 3294(A); BROUSSEAU V. JARRETT (1977) 73 CAL.APP.3D 864, 872.) THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE PURSUANT TO EV. CODE SEC. 452(H) OF THE FACT THAT VANGUARD PROPERTIES WAS A LICENSED CORPORATION THROUGHOUT THE RELEVANT TIME PERIOD ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT AND THEREFORE STRIKES THE ALLEGATIONS IT WAS UNLICENSED PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 436(A). THE OPPOSITION PAPERS DO NOT ADDRESS STRIKING THE ALLEGATIONS PERTAINING TO MS. VO. THEREFORE, THE MOTION TO STRIKE THOSE PORTIONS OF THE FAC IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JUILETTE VO, CHIS JAMES CORPORATION and PAULA GOLD-NOCELLA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The Listing Agreement states that the parties agree to mediate prior to commencing suit or arbitration; however, if a party fails to do so, that party is not entitled to recover attorney's fees. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC"), Ex. A.) Therefore, the contract provides that the remedy for failure to comply with this contractual provision is loss of attorney's fees, not barring the claims. Defendants have not established that Robert Carter is an indispensable party to the suit. Although Mr. Carter executed the Listing Agreement, he executed the contract as an agent on behalf of a principal, Vanguard Properties. When an agent executes a contract on behalf of a disclosed principal, the presumption is that the principal and not the agent is to be bound. (Bank of America Nat. Trust and Savings Ass'n v. Cryer (1936) 6 Cal.2d 485, 489.) Because the FAC seeks relief from Vanguard Properties, as the principal, and not from Mr. Carter, as the agent, Mr. Carter is not an indispensable party. Persons against whom no relief is sought cannot properly be joined as defendants. (Pinnacle Holdings Inc. v. Simon (1995) 31 Cal.Appl.4th 1430, 1437.) Order signed after hearing. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JUILETTE VO, CHIS JAMES CORPORATION and PAULA GOLD-NOCELLA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to Ev. Code sec. 452(h). The following portions of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") are stricken pursuant to CCP sec. 436(a): par. 13, pg. 5, lines 22-24; par. 18, pg. 7, lines 8-14; par. 1, pg. 1, line 27 - pg. 2, line 2; par. 13, pg. 5, line 28 - pg. 6, line 1; par. 18, pg. 7, lines 6-7. The FAC does not plead a statutory or contractual basis for attorney's fees. Additionally, the opposition to the motion to strike did not set forth any argument explaining why the tort of another doctrine applies. The FAC does not plead facts establishing Defendants acted with "oppression, fraud, or malice" to support a prayer for punitive damages. (Civ. Code sec. 3294(a); Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872.) The Court takes judicial notice pursuant to Ev. Code sec. 452(h) of the fact that Vanguard Properties was a licensed corporation throughout the relevant time period alleged in the complaint and therefore strikes the allegations it was unlicensed pursuant to CCP sec. 436(a). The opposition papers do not address striking the allegations pertaining to Ms. Vo. Therefore, the motion to strike those portions of the FAC is granted as unopposed. Order signed after hearing. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
